name: npm-safety-guard
description: Detects malicious npm packages, CVEs, and risky install hooks before installation. Live OSV.dev + supply chain attack DB (Shai-Hulud, Axios/DPRK RAT, Lazarus, event-stream). By SendWaveHub.

commands.title: NPM Safety Guard: Scan package.json Now
NPM Safety Guard: Show Security Report
NPM Safety Guard: Check ReversingLabs (CVE + Malware)
NPM Safety Guard: Scan with OSV.dev (CVEs)
NPM Safety Guard: Fix All CVEs in This File
NPM Safety Guard: Fix All CVEs in Workspace
NPM Safety Guard: Refresh Malware Database
NPM Safety Guard: Audit Install Scripts (preinstall/postinstall)
NPM Safety Guard: Deep Scan All Dependencies (tarball AST)
NPM Safety Guard: Scan Lockfile (full resolved tree)
NPM Safety Guard: Compute Risk Heuristics (age / maintainers / downloads)
NPM Safety Guard: Check Typosquats / Homoglyphs (package names)

npmSafetyGuard.enableAutoScan.description: Automatically scan package.json on open and save

npmSafetyGuard.showInlineDecorations.description: Show inline warning decorations in package.json

npmSafetyGuard.enableOSV.description: Automatically query OSV.dev for CVEs alongside the built-in malware DB. Free, no API key required.

npmSafetyGuard.enableRemoteDb.description: Pull the community malicious-package feed at activation (cached 24h). New attacks light up without an extension update.

npmSafetyGuard.remoteDbUrl.description: Custom URL for the malicious-package feed (JSON array of MaliciousEntry). Leave blank for the official feed.

npmSafetyGuard.enableScriptCheck.description: Audit each dependency's published metadata for preinstall / install / postinstall / prepare scripts. The #1 supply-chain attack vector.

npmSafetyGuard.scriptWhitelist.description: Additional package names to suppress from install-script warnings (e.g. internal tooling). Bundled whitelist already covers bcrypt, sharp, esbuild, husky, prisma, sqlite3, etc.

npmSafetyGuard.flagPrepareHooks.description: Flag packages that only declare a `prepare` script. `prepare` runs on git-URL / local-folder installs, but NOT on normal `npm install <name>` from the registry — so flagging it creates noise for most users. Enable this if you install dependencies from git URLs.

npmSafetyGuard.enableTyposquat.description: Detect typosquatting (Damerau-Levenshtein vs top-package list) and Cyrillic/Greek homoglyph attacks in package names. Instant, offline, zero network.

npmSafetyGuard.rlToken.description: ReversingLabs Spectra Assure Community API token. Get free token at https://secure.software (sign in → profile → create token). Enables real-time CVE and malware detection beyond the built-in static database.
